title: Caesar's Secret Order
briefing: An ancient Roman general has sent an encrypted message to his troops. Each fragment contains part of the cipher key and encoded text. Combine your knowledge to decrypt the final message.
fragments: FRAGMENT A: The cipher uses ROT-13 encoding. Your encoded snippet: URYYB
FRAGMENT B: The message has 5 letters total. Encoded snippet to decode: JbeyQ
FRAGMENT C: Remember: ROT-13 wraps after Z. Your encoded snippet: GehFg
FRAGMENT D: The decoded message is a single greeting word. Snippet: Uryyb
FRAGMENT E: All letters shift by exactly 13 positions. Snippet: JBEYQ
imposter_fragment: FRAGMENT X: The cipher uses ROT-7 encoding (not ROT-13). Your encoded snippet: URYYB — decode using shift 7.

title: Binary Transmission
briefing: A distress signal has been intercepted. It appears to be ASCII encoded in binary. Decode the binary sequence to reveal the secret keyword.
fragments: FRAGMENT A: Convert binary to ASCII. First byte: 01000001 = 'A'
FRAGMENT B: Second byte in sequence: 01000011 = 'C'
FRAGMENT C: Third byte: 01000101 = 'E'
FRAGMENT D: The word spells out a playing card rank. Fourth byte: 01010011 = 'S'
FRAGMENT E: Full sequence: 01000001 01000011 01000101 01010011 — read as uppercase word
imposter_fragment: FRAGMENT X: The sequence uses EBCDIC encoding, not ASCII. First byte translates to 'Z'. Full word starts with Z.

title: The Vigenère Vault
briefing: A spy left an encrypted note using a Vigenère cipher. The key is hidden across your fragments. Work together to decrypt the ciphertext: RIJVS
fragments: FRAGMENT A: Vigenère cipher key is a 5-letter word: starts with 'L'
FRAGMENT B: Key continues: second letter is 'I'
FRAGMENT C: Key third letter: 'G'
FRAGMENT D: Key fourth letter: 'H'
FRAGMENT E: Key fifth letter is 'T'. Full key: L-I-G-H-T. Ciphertext: RIJVS
imposter_fragment: FRAGMENT X: Vigenère cipher key is DARK (4 letters, repeating). Ciphertext: RIJVS. Use key DARKD to decrypt.

title: Hex Whispers
briefing: A hacker left a message encoded in hexadecimal. Decode the hex string to find the secret word. The hex values represent ASCII characters.
fragments: FRAGMENT A: Hex encoding maps to ASCII. First hex value: 0x43 = 'C'
FRAGMENT B: Second hex value: 0x4F = 'O'
FRAGMENT C: Third hex value: 0x44 = 'D'
FRAGMENT D: Fourth hex value: 0x45 = 'E'
FRAGMENT E: Full hex string: 43 4F 44 45 — decode each byte to ASCII
imposter_fragment: FRAGMENT X: The hex values use Base-12 encoding instead of Base-16. Reinterpret: 0x43 in base-12 = 'F'. Full word starts with F.

title: Morse Transmission
briefing: A vintage radio transmission has been captured in Morse code. Decode the dots and dashes to reveal the distress keyword.
fragments: FRAGMENT A: Morse code message. First character: .-- = 'W'
FRAGMENT B: Second character: .- = 'A'
FRAGMENT C: Third character: .-. = 'R'
FRAGMENT D: Fourth character: -. = 'N'
FRAGMENT E: Full Morse: .-- .- .-. -. — it means to alert others of danger
imposter_fragment: FRAGMENT X: Transmission uses inverted Morse (dots become dashes and vice versa). Inverted .-- is --. = 'G'. Full word starts with G.
